steps.title: Identify what the passage needs
steps.content: The passage claims that "not all flying birds are so light" compared to the under-1-kg norm. The blank must complete an example of a flying bird whose mass is dramatically above 1 kg — both conditions matter: the bird must (1) be capable of flight, and (2) have a mass well above the norm according to the table.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — mute swan, which has an average mass of 11.9 kg.: The mute swan is a flying bird and 11.9 kg is far above the under-1-kg baseline — a compelling example for the passage's claim. ✓
  • Choice B — Andean condor, which has an average mass below 10 kg.: Misrepresents the table — the Andean condor's mass is 11.3 kg, not below 10 kg. ✗
  • Choice C — Somali ostrich, which has a higher average mass than the dwarf cassowary.: The Somali ostrich is flightless, so it cannot serve as an example of a flying bird. ✗
  • Choice D — Somali ostrich, which has an average mass of 90.0 kg.: Also a flightless bird — irrelevant to the passage's point about flying species. ✗
A is correct.
